core_concept: Spatial Sense: Coordinates, Hemispheres, and Climate Regions
facts: Latitude lines run horizontally around Earth and measure distance north or south of the equator, which is the 0° line that divides the planet into Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
Longitude lines run vertically from the North Pole to the South Pole and help pinpoint a location's east-west position when combined with latitude.
Climate regions are linked to latitude: the tropics lie near the equator and are warm year-round, while polar regions near the poles are cold because sunlight hits them at a slanted angle.
The equator is the dividing line for hemispheres, not the prime meridian; a location north of the equator is in the Northern Hemisphere, and one south is in the Southern Hemisphere.
misconceptions: Students think the equator is the line that separates east and west hemispheres instead of north and south hemispheres.
Students believe the tropics are the hottest region because they are closest to the sun, not because the sun's rays strike the equator more directly.
